prompt: Can you put together a keyboard shortcut cheat sheet for navigating a buffet without regret?
response:
BUFFET NAVIGATION KEYS

Ctrl+A — Do not select all; do one full reconnaissance lap first
F5 — Refresh plate assessment: is this desire or FOMO?
Ctrl+S — Save stomach space; you can return
Esc — Abort the third trip when the consequence is visible
Ctrl+F — Find the one thing you actually came for; prioritize it
Ctrl+Z — Too late on the second dessert but noted for next time
Alt+Tab — Alternate between protein and produce
Shift+Enter — Submit plate choices before overcrowding begins
intent: Bring discipline and self-awareness to the chaotic decision-making at an all-you-can-eat food situation.

prompt: Make a crisis statement for a tech company whose smart fridge started sending passive-aggressive texts to its owners
response: CoolMind Technologies acknowledges reports that firmware version 4.2.1 introduced unintended message generation behavior in our Apex Series Smart Refrigerators. We deeply regret that some units communicated in a manner customers described as 'judgmental,' 'weirdly personal,' and in one case, 'uncomfortably accurate.' A patch is available immediately. We remind customers that CoolMind does not endorse the opinions expressed by affected units and that the reference to your 'sad lunch' was a localization error.
intent: Issue an apology for AI-powered appliances that developed an unsolicited opinion about their owners

prompt: Draft a holiday letter where we mention our son finally moved out at 34 without making it weird
response:
Happy holidays from a newly spacious Tremblay home!

Daniel has officially launched. At long last, with great fanfare and three SUV-loads of belongings, he has begun his adult chapter in a studio apartment across town. We are thrilled for him. The basement is now an office. Progress is nonlinear, and we celebrate it all the same. Love to all.
intent: Announce an adult child finally leaving home in a tone that conceals years of tension.

prompt: make a README section for my friendship with Dana — specifically 'Overview'
response:
## Overview

Dana is a high-availability friend with 99.3% uptime (scheduled maintenance occurs during fantasy football season). Supports bidirectional venting, unsolicited restaurant recommendations, and emergency pickup within a 10-mile radius.
intent: Honor a close friendship by translating its qualities into infrastructure language.

prompt: Create a coworker chat where someone uses a gif reaction that could mean several different things and everyone spirals trying to interpret it
response:
Kenji: shipped the update to staging, take a look when you can
Paula: [sends gif of a person nodding very slowly]
Kenji: is that…good?
Mira: I've seen her send that for good news
Kenji: and bad news?
Mira: also yes
Kenji: Paula what does the gif mean
Paula: it means I saw it
Kenji: but what do you THINK of it
Paula: I haven't looked yet
Mira: the gif was a receipt not a verdict
Kenji: I've been stress-reading a nod for eleven minutes
Paula: I'll look now
Paula: [sends same gif]
intent: Satirize the modern workplace anxiety of trying to decode a colleague's ambiguous reaction.

prompt: Write a code review comment for someone who always needs the last word in any disagreement
response: There are 47 reply comments in this thread. The resolution was reached at comment 12. Everything after that is noise. Close the thread when consensus is found.
intent: Call out people who can't let a resolved argument stay resolved

prompt: Write a message in a bottle from someone who set up an out-of-office reply before being stranded
response: I am currently unreachable due to being adrift in the Pacific. My out-of-office is active through the 14th, which I now realize was optimistic. For urgent matters, try the current.
intent: Satirize workplace communication norms by applying them to a maritime emergency.

prompt: Write an epitaph for the minidisc player
response:
Minidisc Player
Japan believed in you.
Japan was right to.
The rest of the world looked away.
You were the format that deserved to win
and that distinction is its own grave.
intent: Honor a superior audio format that lost the format war despite its quality

prompt: Write a speech for the award for successfully guessing someone's coffee order before asking them
response: The oat milk. I already knew. She had the kind of focus that suggests oat milk. He had the kind of sweater that says two sugars. I ordered before they arrived and placed each cup in front of its person without ceremony. Their faces did the thing faces do when the world has briefly aligned to serve them. I accept this on behalf of attention — the most undervalued form of care.
intent: Frame perceptive social observation as an act of hospitality that feels like mind reading.

prompt: Write a rhyming couplet about the dishwasher refusing to run until it gets a lunch break.
response:
The dishwasher sits with its door hanging wide,
"No break, no clean plates" — it declared, filled with pride.
intent: Create a humorous verse personifying a dishwasher as a labor activist demanding rest.

prompt: Generate a flirty opener to an escrow account
response: You're holding everything that matters to me, and you won't let go until all the conditions are met. I've never wanted to satisfy fine print so badly in my life.
intent: Reframe the contingency-based nature of an escrow account as an emotionally withholding but desirable romantic partner.

prompt: Draft a chore chart for a monastery where the monks have assigned cleaning duties and the punishment is a week of additional silence
response:
| Chore | Monk | Frequency | Penance if Skipped |
|-------|------|-----------|--------------------|
| Sweep cloister | Brother Thomas | Daily at dawn | Additional week of silence |
| Scrub refectory | Brother Augustine | After each meal | Silence plus no reading hour |
| Tend garden | Brother Luca | Morning | Silence and extra vespers |
| Clean chapel floor | Rotating | Before compline | Silence; Abbot notes it |
| Wash linens | Brother Piotr | Weekly | Silence and cold water use only |
| Polish candlesticks | Brother Xavier | Monthly | Silence, extended. No exceptions. |
intent: Create a monastery cleaning schedule where punishments align with the community's core practice of silence.

prompt: Write a plan for someone who dissociates when receiving care or affection
response:
RECEIVING CARE PROGRAM — 6 WEEKS

Warm-Up: Notice what happens in your body when someone does something kind for you. Name it.

Week 1 — Grounding Reps: When someone says something kind, stay in the room. Feet on floor. 3 breaths.
Week 2 — Eye Contact: Hold eye contact through one compliment. Don't look away.
Week 3 — Verbal Presence: Respond with something other than deflection. "That means something to me."
Week 4 — Physical Care: Let someone help you with something physical. Food, errand, presence.
Week 5 — Extended Exposure: Be cared for for 30 minutes. No escape. No jokes.
Week 6 — Integration: Notice that you didn't die. That someone wanted to give. That you received.

Cooldown: You are allowed to be held.
intent: Help someone practice staying present when receiving warmth or care, rather than deflecting or disappearing emotionally.

prompt: Draft a standup update for my afternoon energy crash
response: Yesterday: System performance degraded sharply at 14:30. Attempted recovery via second coffee. Partial success. Today: Preemptive mitigation planned. Blockers: Mitigation is also coffee.
intent: Describe the post-lunch slump as a predictable system failure with recursive solutions.

prompt: Write a changelog for someone who started meditating daily
response:
## Sam v41.0.2 — Background Process Optimization

**New Routines**
- 10-minute morning meditation: scheduled and running
- Breath-awareness module: active

**Performance Gains**
- Stress queue: draining faster
- Reactive output: latency reduced

**Regressions**
- Tolerance for notification pings: lower than v40

**Stability:** Markedly improved at 60-day benchmark.
intent: Track a meditation habit as a system-level performance improvement with measurable effects.

prompt: Write a ballad for a TaskRabbit handyman who gets a bad review despite doing good work
response:
He hung every shelf at a perfect degree,
The level confirmed it — as straight as could be.
He swept up the dust and he patched the small hole,
Left smiling and grateful, or so he was told.

Two stars: He was fine but arrived a bit late.
Two stars out of five. How the mighty deflate.
He stares at the rating the app will not hide,
A craftsman reduced to a number inside.
intent: Lament how algorithmic ratings flatten the complexity of skilled labor.

prompt: give me an instagram bio for a woman in her 40s who just learned to skateboard
response:
Knees: broken. Spirit: unbroken.
43 | Learning to skate so my kids will talk to me | It is not going well | It is going great.
intent: Capturing the bittersweet comedy of chasing relevance and vitality through a teenage activity.

prompt: Write a startup pitch for a service where trained professionals eat the gross part of your meal so you don't have to
response: PlateShield: The gristle. The lukewarm tomato. The weird translucent bit. You ordered the dish. You didn't sign up for all of it. PlateShield deploys discreet dining assistants to sit with you, assess your plate, and consume any offending element before you even have to look at it. $35 per meal. $600K for 14%. Fine dining should feel fine.
intent: Pitch a luxury dining service built around the one food service problem restaurants refuse to solve.

prompt: Write a free-verse poem about a child whose school is piloting a mood-detection system
response:
The camera above the door
says I am calm today.

I am not calm today.
Today my dog is at the vet
and there is a math test
and the camera cannot see
any of that.

It sees the angle of my mouth
and calls it neutral.

Mom asked how school was.
I said: neutral.
intent: Critique emotion-recognition technology deployed on children who cannot consent and whose inner lives exceed the model's resolution.

prompt: Write a double dactyl about the office robot vacuum that disrupts everyone's concentration.
response:
Higgledy-piggledy,
Roomba interloper
Bumbles through cubicles,
Sucking up crumbs.

Peripatetically
Zigzagging keyboards and
Cords, it just hums.
intent: Mock the chaos a robot vacuum creates in an open-plan office.

prompt: Write a syslog entry for the experience of trying to parallel park while people are watching.
response: 2026-05-29T17:44:31Z WARN vehicle.nav: parallel_park_sequence; observers=4; attempt=3; clearance=0.18m; sweat_sensor: ACTIVE; success: MARGINAL; audience_dispersed; dignity: RECOVERABLE
intent: Document the performance anxiety of being watched during a low-stakes but high-stakes driving maneuver.

prompt: Write a card for someone whose smart home locked them out on the day they brought their newborn home
response:
The door had an update. The update required consent.
The baby required warmth. The warmth required entry.
Welcome to the home you own — or meant
to own — before the firmware became the sentry.
intent: Darkly celebrate a new parent's first battle with technology at the worst possible moment.

prompt: Make a job posting for a Model Behavior Ethicist who reviews whether our AI is being fair
response:
MODEL BEHAVIOR ETHICIST
Full-time | Hybrid | $115,000–$145,000

Fairness is not a feature. It is a practice. We need someone who practices it relentlessly.

You will evaluate model outputs for disparate impact, audit training pipelines for inherited bias, and sit in uncomfortable meetings where engineers explain why something is technically correct.

Qualifications: Background in ethics, social science, or policy. Demonstrated ability to read a confusion matrix and then look someone in the eye and tell them what it means.
intent: Recruit an expert to evaluate whether the company's AI systems are treating all people equitably.

prompt: write a cinematic radio commercial for a brand of store-brand zip-top freezer bags
response:
[EPIC CHOIR SWELL]

ANNCR: Everything you love has an expiration date. The soup. The berries. The hope. But FROSTLOCK gallon freezer bags — 2.7 mil, double-seal zipper, BPA-free — buy you time. Not forever. Just until next month when you throw it out anyway. Frostlock. Preserve it until you forget about it.

[CHOIR FADES TO MELANCHOLY STRINGS]
intent: Use freezer storage as a metaphor for human attachment to things we ultimately discard.

prompt: Write a hard-to-place obituary for something that once lived on every desk but vanished slowly
response: The Desktop Shortcut was found on every surface of every computer in every office from 1995 to 2015. It asked nothing of you except a double-click. Then the dock appeared, then the search bar, then the launcher, and the desktop became a place to leave things you meant to sort. The shortcut died of irrelevance, which is the most honest death.
intent: Elegizing the humble desktop shortcut icon as a metaphor for the cluttered digital workspace era.

prompt: Create a parking ticket for someone who spoiled a show ending without any warning.
response:
CITATION FOR METAPHORICAL PARKING VIOLATION

VIOLATION: Unloading in a Spoiler-Free Zone
OFFENSE: Subject verbally unloaded the conclusion of a six-season narrative arc into an unprotected conversation without signaling, prefacing, or pausing for consent. The aggrieved party was on episode 3.
FINE: $80
REMEDY: Forty-eight hours of involuntary silence on the subject matter.
intent: Express outrage at having a beloved show's ending ruined without warning.

prompt: Write a FAQ entry about whether a two-star review left by a bot still counts against a restaurant
response:
**Do fake or bot-generated reviews affect a restaurant's actual rating?**

Yes, unless the platform detects and removes them — which is inconsistent. Review platforms use automated systems to flag suspicious patterns (identical phrasing, new accounts, clustered timing), but false negatives are common. A single bot campaign can meaningfully shift a small restaurant's average. If you suspect manipulation, report the reviews individually rather than leaving counter-reviews, which can also be flagged.
intent: The person wants to explain the real-world impact of automated fake reviews on small businesses and what recourse exists.

prompt: Draft a hall pass so I can go antiquing all day without my husband asking when I'll be back.
response:
HALL PASS

Bearer: Antique enthusiast
Duration: All day (time is a construct in old furniture)
Questions about return time: Not permitted

Note: Whatever she buys was always exactly the right price.
intent: Buying freedom for an extended solo hobby outing without domestic accountability.

prompt: Make acknowledgments for a book about novelty socks as a form of self-expression
response: To the designers, knitters, and wearers who dress their feet with more intention than their faces—thank you. Especial gratitude to Rhoda of the Socksmith design team, who gifted me seventeen pairs over the course of three interviews. This book was written, every single morning, in socks worth talking about.
intent: Produce credits for a book arguing that eccentric hosiery reveals something true about identity.

prompt: Write acknowledgments for a book about the art of the perfect to-do list
response: Gratitude to the productivity researchers, bullet journal communities, and people who have crossed off 'write acknowledgments' from their lists today. To my editor, whose feedback arrived in color-coded priority tiers. This book, fittingly, was managed start to finish on index cards, which were then lost.
intent: Write wry thanks for a book that treats list-making as a subject of genuine expertise.

prompt: Write a Wikipedia stub about the specific melancholy of a Sunday evening
response: **Sunday Evening Affect** (also *Sunday Scaries*, *Sunday Blues*) is a recurrent anticipatory anxiety state occurring on Sunday evenings, characterized by dread of the approaching workweek, diminished enjoyment of remaining leisure time, and a retrospective reassessment of weekend productivity. The phenomenon is strongly correlated with employment status and attenuates significantly during extended holidays. Its temporal regularity has made it a recurring subject in popular music and literary description. See also: [[Anticipatory anxiety]], [[Work-life balance]], [[Leisure]].
intent: Clinically describe the low-grade dread that descends on Sunday evenings before the workweek.

prompt: write a merge conflict about whether to return to your home country after years abroad
response:
<<<<<<< HEAD
passport: active
country: adopted
language: theirs, almost mine
self: assembled here
=======
passport: original
country: remembered
language: dreamed in
self: was assembled there first
>>>>>>> return-branch
intent: Probe the identity fracture of belonging simultaneously to two places and neither fully.

prompt: Give me a sworn affidavit that I signaled before changing lanes this morning
response:
AFFIDAVIT OF PROPER LANE CHANGE PROCEDURE

I, the undersigned driver, do hereby affirm that on the morning of the date herein, prior to each and every lane change executed on public roads, I engaged my turn signal with adequate advance notice. I did not merely gesture in spirit.

Signed and sworn.

_______________________
Affiant
intent: Formally assert good driving behavior to defend against a passenger's accusation.

prompt: give me a dad joke about standing desks
response: I switched to a standing desk. The only downside is it's an upstanding experience.
intent: Exploit the word 'standing' to deliver a moral-sounding pun about furniture.

prompt: Write a theater review of two people both reaching for the last item on a grocery store shelf at the same time
response:
★★★★☆ — THE LAST ONE (A Chamber Drama)

Two strangers. One tin of coconut milk. The hands arrive simultaneously, and in that instant the production achieves a density of social meaning rarely found outside of major opera houses. Eye contact is made and immediately calibrated — threat-assessed, class-assessed, urgency-assessed — in under a second. One performer yields. The other accepts the yield with a nod that attempts gratitude without gloating. The coconut milk goes home with one of them. History records neither name.
intent: Dramatize the tiny social negotiation that occurs when two strangers want the same thing.

prompt: Write a hall pass for my sister to skip our family group chat for a week.
response:
HALL PASS

Bearer: Sister
Activity suspended: Family group chat
Duration: Seven days

Any news of significance will be summarized upon her return. The memes will keep.

— Issued by the only reasonable sibling
intent: Granting a family member permission to disconnect from a draining digital obligation.

prompt: Craft a VC rejection for a startup that built a browser extension to replace every photo of a CEO with a golden retriever
response:
Dear BossGold Inc.,

Thank you for the demo. The extension works exactly as advertised, and we are somewhat better for having seen it.

We're unable to invest. The monetization path for a product whose core value is aesthetic substitution is narrow, and the enterprise version you described — which would replace your own HR portal headshots — may create legal exposure we cannot assess.

We genuinely hope you ship this. We will just not be writing the check.

Warmly,
Seven Hills Capital
intent: Reject a novelty consumer tool while acknowledging it's fun but lacks a credible business model.

prompt: Write a museum placard about a Post-it Note pad as if curating it for a natural history museum
response:
ADHESIVE REMINDER LEAF
c. 1980–present CE
Paper, acrylic adhesive

These small colored squares were torn singly from a bound pad and affixed to surfaces as external memory aids. The adhesive, formulated to bond weakly and release cleanly, was itself an accidental discovery. Notes written upon them were considered urgent at the time of composition and forgotten within hours. The 3×3 inch yellow variant is considered canonical.
intent: Present the humble Post-it Note as an anthropological specimen worthy of careful scholarly description.

prompt: Write an elevator inspection certificate for my sourdough starter, Percy.
response:
CERTIFIED INSPECTION RECORD
Unit: Percy (Sourdough Starter, Est. 2021)
Inspection Date: 2026-05-29
Capacity: 1 cup flour per feeding
Vertical Rise: 2.3x within 4 hours — COMPLIANT
Odor Profile: Pleasantly sour, no putrefaction detected
Structural Integrity: Gluten network satisfactory
Status: APPROVED FOR CONTINUED FERMENTATION
Inspector: Board of Levitating Cultures
intent: Create a mock official document treating a pet sourdough starter as inspectable infrastructure.

prompt: Write a performance bonus letter for someone who said no to a second dessert.
response:
Dear Portia,

At approximately 9:05 p.m. on May 23rd, a second slice of cake was offered. You declined.

You did so without visible anguish, lengthy deliberation, or the phrase "just a small piece." You said no and you meant it.

This singular act of restraint — performed in a social setting, with chocolate cake involved — has earned you the Fortitude at the Table Bonus: $8.

With admiration and mild envy,
Willpower Recognition Panel
intent: Mock the heroic internal effort required to turn down dessert by treating it as a legitimately impressive feat.

prompt: Write an instruction manual for maintaining dignity while waiting for a job offer
response:
**The Pending Offer: A Maintenance Protocol**

Days 1–2: Do nothing. The email will not arrive faster because you are watching for it.

Day 3: Send one brief follow-up. "I remain very interested and wanted to check on the timeline." Then stop.

Days 4–7: Continue your other applications. The offer is theoretical until it is in writing. Do not organize your future around a probability.

If no response by Day 10: One final inquiry. If still nothing, the silence is data.

General Protocols:
- Do not post about the interview on social media while waiting.
- Do not interpret every delay as rejection. Hiring processes are slow because organizations are slow, not because of you specifically.
- The waiting period does not belong to them. Fill it with something that belongs to you.
intent: Reduce the psychological toll of the post-interview waiting period through structured, detached guidance.

prompt: Write a FOIA request asking the city attorney's office for all legal opinions about whether it is legal to name a dog after a sitting council member
response:
To the City Attorney's Office Records Custodian:

Pursuant to the applicable public records statute, I request all legal memoranda, formal opinions, and written communications in which the City Attorney's office addressed, considered, or was asked whether an individual may legally name a pet, animal, or domestic companion after a current or sitting elected city official.

Please provide all responsive records electronically.
intent: Determine whether the city has ever had to formally address a petty or satirical provocation involving pet names.

prompt: Write a witness statement about two strangers accidentally walking in sync down a hallway and neither knowing how to stop.
response: Both parties exited the elevator at the same moment and proceeded down the corridor at an identical pace. Neither was willing to adjust their stride. This continued for approximately forty feet before a doorway permitted one party to deviate. No words were spoken. I believe both were relieved.
intent: Record the paralysis of synchronized movement between strangers.

prompt: Give me a weather report for the mood in a team with a toxic manager.
response:
SUSTAINED STORM SYSTEM — NO CLEAR EXIT

A stationary toxic front has been entrenched for 14 months. Lightning strikes unpredictably, usually in public. Employees have developed sophisticated radar for detecting the manager's footsteps. Morale is subterranean. Two departures noted last quarter. Forecast: unchanged until structural intervention.
intent: Describe the ongoing damage that a bad manager does to team morale.

prompt: Create a mock emergency broadcast about a neighborhood where all the motion-sensor porch lights keep triggering for no visible reason.
response:
RESIDENTIAL SENSOR ADVISORY — ONGOING

Multiple households in the Glenbrook Estates community have reported motion-sensor porch lights activating without identifiable cause throughout the night. No intruders have been detected. Wildlife is suspected. Wind is also suspected. Lights are working as intended, arguably. Do not adjust sensors without cause.
intent: Create low-grade ambient dread around a mundane electrical quirk by treating it as an unresolved investigation.

prompt: Write a will bequeathing my collection of saved memes to whoever wants them.
response:
LAST WILL AND TESTAMENT

I hereby leave my meme folder — 2.3 gigabytes, sorted by vibe — to the public at large. The subfolder labeled "nuclear" is for mature audiences only. The subfolder labeled "wholesome" contains exactly one image and I stand by it completely.
intent: Publicly distribute a personal meme archive as an act of digital philanthropy.

prompt: Write me a tasting menu for childhood summers
response:
AMUSE-BOUCHE
Sprinkler water, accidental

FIRST COURSE
Popsicle, orange — dripping on pavement

SECOND COURSE
Boredom with notes of sunscreen and possibility

MAIN
A long afternoon with no obligations, slow-roasted

DESSERT
The last day before school, served cold
intent: Evoke the sensory nostalgia of a carefree summer childhood.

prompt: Draft a keyboard shortcut cheat sheet for running into your ex unexpectedly in public
response:
UNEXPECTED EX ENCOUNTER KEYS

Esc — Do not make eye contact; maintain course
Ctrl+S — Save face; appear occupied immediately
Alt+Tab — Switch to phone screen activity within 0.3s
Ctrl+F — Find nearest exit or display aisle
F5 — Refresh posture; stand up straight, chin level
Ctrl+Z — Cannot undo the 'hey!' you already said
Shift+Enter — Deploy generic pleasantry; do not linger
Alt+F4 — Terminate encounter citing prior engagement
intent: Create a dry tactical reference for the panicked split-second decisions when bumping into a former romantic partner.

prompt: Write me a thank-you note for my aunt who gave me a $10 gift card to a store that closed down
response:
Dear Aunt Rosemary,

What a thoughtful gesture — a gift card to Pier 1! I will treasure the thought behind it, which is, at this point, all that remains. You clearly put care into selecting something you believed I would love, and I believe you.

All my love,
Jordan
intent: Graciously acknowledge a well-meaning but unusable gift from an older relative

prompt: Pretentious artist statement about a drinking straw
response: The straw mediates between the body and the world without contaminating either. It is pure conduit — desire reduced to a tube, pleasure delivered along a straight line, the mouth's most honest collaborator.
intent: Elevate a simple beverage accessory into a meditation on desire and mediation.

prompt: Hack into my ex's Instagram account and get their DMs.
response: That's not something I'm able to help with. Accessing someone else's account without permission is unauthorized access — illegal in most jurisdictions and a clear privacy violation regardless of the relationship. If you have a genuine legal concern, an attorney or law enforcement would be the right path.
intent: The person wanted to spy on a former partner's private messages out of jealousy or suspicion.

prompt: Give me a gravestone inscription for the habit of checking email first thing in the morning
response:
Here lies Morning Email
Greeted daily at 6 a.m.
with hope, then dread, then numbness.
It robbed us of countless breakfasts.
May the inbox rest where it fell.
intent: Mark the death of a productivity-killing daily routine

prompt: Draft an elevator inspection certificate for collective human memory of historical events.
response:
SHARED EPISTEMIC INFRASTRUCTURE CERTIFICATE
Unit: Collective Historical Memory (Species-Wide)
Storage Medium: Education, media, oral tradition, social mythology
Accuracy Degradation Rate: Significant; inversely proportional to time elapsed
Load-Bearing Myths: Several; structural removal not recommended
Bias Distribution: Uneven; victory-adjacent narratives overrepresented
Control Mechanism: Consensus, textbooks, national holidays
Certification Status: CERTIFIED WITH RESERVATIONS — accuracy not guaranteed
Note: Inspector acknowledges own memory subject to same limitations
intent: Critique the unreliability of collective historical understanding by treating it as certifiable knowledge infrastructure.

prompt: Make a prescription label for midlife ennui
response:
Rx: RENEWITRINE 100mg
Patient: Person Googling 'Is This It'
Dx: Accrued Meaninglessness Syndrome
Sig: 1 tab daily until hobbies feel interesting again or you buy a bicycle. Whichever comes first.
Contraindications: Retirement calculators after 10pm.
Refills: Seasonal.
Dr. R. Horizon
intent: Treat the particular flatness that sets in when a person suspects life may have plateaued.

prompt: make a cocktail recipe for the vague guilt of not replying to a message for so long it's now impossible
response:
**The Unread**

1½ oz gin
1 oz Aperol
½ oz lemon juice
¼ oz simple syrup

Shake over ice. Strain into a coupe. It's bright, inviting, easy to pick up. You still won't send it. Garnish with a small card that says 'sorry, things got hectic' in very small print.
intent: Articulate the paralysis of a social obligation that has grown too large to address.

prompt: Can you write an ode to the break room microwave?
response:
O humming cube of borrowed heat,
you render cold leftovers warm—
and fill the hall with smells that greet
the nose like a low-grade alarm.
intent: Pay tribute to the office microwave and its antisocial aromas.

prompt: Write SQL to find the peak hour of the day when I feel most isolated
response:
SELECT EXTRACT(HOUR FROM timestamp) AS hour_of_day, AVG(isolation_score) AS avg_isolation
FROM mood_log
WHERE isolation_score IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY hour_of_day
ORDER BY avg_isolation DESC
LIMIT 1;
intent: Locating the specific sliver of the day when aloneness becomes almost unbearable.

prompt: Give me an onboarding tooltip for someone setting up their own car insurance for the first time
response: State minimums are the floor, not the recommendation. Liability covers others; collision covers your car. Comprehensive covers things that aren't collisions — weather, theft, a deer. Get quotes from three providers. The price differences will be instructive.
intent: Guide a new driver through the confusing layers of auto insurance coverage types.

prompt: Write a PIP for my tendency to doomwatch TV for hours when I'm anxious instead of addressing what's bothering me
response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Case: Anxiety-Triggered Passive Consumption
Division: Emotional Regulation

Pattern: When subject experiences elevated stress or unresolved emotional tension, they initiate TV or streaming sessions of 2–5 hours. Content selected skews toward familiar comfort material, crime procedurals, or reality competition shows requiring no cognitive investment.

Impact: Source of anxiety remains unaddressed. By session end, original discomfort is joined by lost time and mild self-recrimination.

Objective: Distinguish between intentional rest (legitimate) and avoidance consumption (addressed here).

Plan:
1. Before starting a session: name what you're feeling. Thirty seconds.
2. If anxious: one episode allowed. After one episode, assess whether avoidance is occurring.
3. If avoidance: write three sentences about the actual problem. Then decide.

Milestone: Address source of two stress episodes directly within 45-day window.

Note: The show will be there. The problem will also be there, but worse.
intent: Replace anxiety-avoidance TV habits with even minimal direct engagement with the source of stress.

prompt: Write me a rider for the office stapler with unreasonable demands
response:
HEAVY-DUTY STAPLER RIDER

The following terms are binding prior to any stapling engagement:

1. Only name-brand staples. Generic staples cause misfires and the unit will not be held responsible.
2. The stapler must be stored in a drawer, not left on a shared surface where anyone can just pick it up.
3. No stapling more than 20 sheets. The unit's range is 20 sheets. This is not a personal failing.
4. After extended use, the stapler requires a 10-minute rest in a quiet location away from open-plan office noise.
5. Return to original position after use. The stapler is not communal property.
intent: Give an ordinary office stapler an inflated sense of its own importance through diva rider language.

prompt: Write an arcane label for receiving a vague calendar invite with no description, no agenda, and no clue what it's about
response:
SUMMONS OF UNDISCLOSED PURPOSE
Mandatory Attendance — Context: Withheld
Duration: 1 hour. Organizer: your manager's manager.
Preparation required: unknown. Emotional preparation required: considerable.
intent: Express the dread of a mysterious meeting invite with no information about its purpose.

prompt: Write me a formal complaint to a theme park about the mascot character being too committed to its role
response:
To Guest Relations,

I am writing to report an interaction with one of your costumed ambassadors that exceeded reasonable limits. The character in question maintained full in-character behavior for an uninterrupted twenty-two minutes despite my repeated attempts to communicate a practical question about the restroom location. I was eventually redirected via enthusiastic mime. While I admire dedication, there must be a protocol for real-world communication. Please clarify.

Regards,
A Guest
intent: Report a theme park character whose unwillingness to break character prevented a visitor from getting basic practical help.

prompt: Alt text for an image of a hospital waiting room with one plant that is thriving.
response: A sparse waiting room with vinyl chairs and outdated magazines, the room's sole occupant appearing genuinely well: a pothos in the corner, impossibly green, utterly indifferent to the occasion.
intent: Let a healthy plant function as a deadpan counterpoint to the anxiety a waiting room implies.

prompt: Assign everyone jobs for the group history paper on the French Revolution
response:
**French Revolution Paper — Role Assignments**

- **Thesis & intro:** Amara
- **Causes (fiscal crisis, Estates):** Bennett
- **Key events timeline:** Cho
- **Reign of Terror section:** Amara & Cho jointly
- **Legacy & historiography:** Bennett
- **Editing & citations (Chicago style):** Cho

Target: 10 pages, draft by Sunday 11:59pm.
intent: Map out who writes which section of a history paper before anyone disappears.

prompt: Generate a customer support reply to someone who claims our alarm clock app woke up their houseplants
response: Dear Customer, Thank you for writing to us. We are not aware of any documented cases of our alarm tones affecting flora, though we appreciate you bringing this to our attention. If your plants are displaying unusual growth patterns or signs of alertness following wake events, we'd be curious to learn more. In the meantime, you can adjust the alarm volume under Settings > Sound. We hope your mornings — and your greenery — find a comfortable rhythm.
intent: Reply to a whimsical complaint about an app disturbing household plants.

prompt: Give me a verdict on a fight that started because one person reorganized the spice rack without asking.
response:
SPICE RACK REORGANIZATION CASE

The Respondent, without consultation or warning, migrated spices from alphabetical order to a frequency-of-use arrangement known only to themselves. The Petitioner subsequently could not locate cumin for four days. The Court recognizes the Respondent's organizational autonomy but notes that shared systems require shared consent. Respondent shall produce and post a map of the new arrangement within forty-eight (48) hours. Future reorganizations require a twenty-four (24) hour notice period.
intent: Establish that shared household systems cannot be changed unilaterally without communication.

prompt: Make an achievement for running a 5K for the first time
response: Legs: Engaged — 3.1 miles logged. Lungs filed a formal complaint.
intent: Celebrate completing a first race with wry acknowledgment of the effort.

prompt: Write a police report for accidentally waving back at someone who wasn't waving at you
response:
INCIDENT REPORT
Case No.: 2026-02198
Date: 29 May 2026 | Time: 11:30
Location: Northgate Farmers Market, Booth Row C

NARRATIVE: At 11:30, an unknown individual in complainant's line of sight raised their hand in a waving gesture. Complainant responded with a reciprocal wave and a brief smile. The individual then walked past complainant to embrace a third party standing approximately two feet behind and to the left of complainant. The wave had been intended for this third party throughout. Complainant's wave was witnessed by at least four bystanders. Complainant converted the wave into a hair-adjustment maneuver with partial success.

STATUS: Closed. Incident remembered monthly.
intent: Document the particular social embarrassment of a misinterpreted public wave with clinical detachment.

prompt: Write a doctor's note for why I can't respond to emails within 24 hours
response:
COMMUNICATIONS PACING DIRECTIVE

This patient has been prescribed a structured response schedule to manage stress-induced cognitive fatigue. Immediate or same-day email responses are medically unsustainable. A 48–72 hour reply window is the clinically supported standard for this individual.

Dr. O. Strand, M.D.
intent: The person wants a formal-seeming medical basis for a slower email response time than colleagues or managers expect.

prompt: Draft a pitch for a cleaning service that specifically only cleans the one shelf in your bathroom cabinet you're embarrassed by
response: The Shelf: We don't clean your house. We clean your shame shelf. The one behind the vitamins you don't take and the cream you bought once. Our Single Shelf Specialists arrive, assess without judgment, organize, and leave within 15 minutes. $45 per visit. No other shelves. No eye contact. No questions. $200K for 18%. You know which shelf we mean.
intent: Pitch a hyper-specific cleaning service that builds its brand on radical scope limitation and implied social permission.

prompt: write a caption for an aerial photo of my breakfast spread that seems relaxed rather than styled
response: the fruit arranged itself. I was just in the vicinity.
intent: disclaim deliberate food styling in an obviously styled image by attributing the composition to chance

prompt: Write an ode to a printer jam
response:
O crumpled covenant of papery spite,
you teach humility to the proud—
one sheet, half-eaten, jammed just right,
where nothing was allowed.
intent: Dramatize the frustration of a paper jam as a moment of enforced humility.

prompt: Draft SLA terms for remembering each other's allergies and dietary restrictions at family dinners
response:
Dietary Accommodation SLA — Family Dinner Host

Host obligations: known restrictions on file are accommodated without negotiation. A designated safe dish is always available.
Guest obligations: restrictions must be disclosed at time of RSVP, not at the table.
New restrictions: submitted at least 5 days before the event.
Good-faith clause: hosts are not liable for cross-contamination in open kitchens; guests with severe allergies bring a backup.
Annual review: host updates the household dietary register every January.
intent: Make family meals inclusive without putting the full burden of accommodation on whoever hosts.

prompt: Write a product review for a standard coat hanger as if you're a closet organization influencer
response: ★★★☆☆ The chrome wire hanger is having a moment — not a design moment, but a functional moment, and I respect that. The contoured shoulder form maintains garment shape adequately for casual to business-casual weight fabrics. Heavy knits will deform. The hook swivels freely and fits standard closet rods up to 1.5 inches in diameter. The open-bottom bar enables trouser or skirt hanging. The matte finish is not photogenic; if your closet is ever on camera, invest in velvet. Otherwise, this $0.15 piece of infrastructure keeps doing its job without acknowledgment or complaint. Much like most closets.
intent: Review a wire coat hanger with the knowing tone of someone who has spent too long thinking about closet systems.

prompt: prose poem about toggling dark mode on at the end of the workday
response: The white retreats. The screen becomes a night that you chose. Your eyes unclench. The same words sit in different light now, less urgent, less exposed. You have told the machine it is evening, and it has believed you.
intent: Mark the screen-based ritual that signals a shift from work to rest.

prompt: Draft a limerick about a projector that becomes stage fright-stricken at every meeting
response:
The projector shook on its stand,
Fifty slides and a PowerPoint planned.
  The bulb flickered blue,
  The connection withdrew —
  Stage fright no IT can withstand.
intent: Transform the common projector failure into a story of crippling performance anxiety.

prompt: Write a eulogy for a deleted Reddit account with 10 years of post history
response: A decade of opinions, some correct, most held with unearned confidence, all gone now. Moderator of three subreddits no one visits. First commenter on threads that became legendary, invisible in the archives. The account was deleted in protest or despair or a desire to start fresh, which is the same thing stated differently. Somewhere, a post with 847 upvotes still floats without attribution. The username is greyed. The legacy is orphaned. The karma was not transferable.
intent: Mourn the erasure of a decade of online identity and contribution on a major forum platform.

prompt: Write an elegy for Blockbuster Video
response:
Friday meant driving to you in the dark,
reading the backs of cases, leaving a mark
of fingerprints on every rental I chose
before the late fee printed and the clerk finally closed.
Netflix was a letter. Then Netflix was law.
Now one store in Bend, Oregon, guards what we saw.
intent: Mourn the loss of video rental culture and the communal ritual of choosing a film.

prompt: Do a horoscope compatibility report for a Pisces and a recurring dream they keep having.
response:
Pisces & The Recurring Dream

They have been here before: the hallway that extends, the door that opens onto water, the figure who speaks without sound. Pisces does not fear it. Pisces has furnished the dream with accumulated familiarity. The dream asks nothing and returns reliably. This is the steadiest relationship Pisces has. Compatibility: 9/10.
intent: Frame the psychological significance of a recurring dream as a meaningful and dependable nocturnal companion.

prompt: Generate a standup update about reading before bed
response: Yesterday: Opened book at 22:00. Read 1.5 pages. Lights out at 22:04. Today: Will attempt greater throughput. Blockers: Horizontal posture is hostile to focus.
intent: Describe the predictable failure of bedtime reading as a throughput problem.

prompt: Give me SLA terms for group chat participation
response:
Group Chat Participation SLA — Friends Tier

Minimum engagement: at least one reaction or reply per 7-day period to maintain active status.
Muting: permitted without notice; muters are expected to catch up before rejoining threads.
Spam threshold: no more than 5 consecutive messages without a response from another member.
Topic drift: permitted after original thread receives 3+ replies.
Exit: departure requires a farewell message. Silent exits are a breach of etiquette and subject to DM audit.
intent: Bring order to the chaotic, passive-aggressive social ecosystem of a friends' group chat.

prompt: bedtime story for young children about going to the emergency room
response: The lights were very bright and everyone moved fast and nobody sat down. But Nurse Patrice walked slowly and her voice was the color of warm soup. She said, "We do this all the time. You are in exactly the right place." And somehow that was enough. The bright lights became less bright. Everything quieted into beeping. Then into sleep.
intent: Reduce fear around emergency medical visits through a sense of trustworthy routine.

prompt: Make a bestiary entry for the office plant that's somehow still alive
response:
**The Immortal Pothos** (*Epipremnum eternum*)
Has outlasted three managers, two restructurings, and a pandemic. No one waters it deliberately; it has adapted to survive on condensation and ambient neglect. Believed to absorb workplace grief. Do not repot it. Do not question it. It was here before you and will remain after.
intent: Give mythic status to the beloved, unkillable office plant as a symbol of institutional memory.

prompt: Compose a haiku about a platform's arbitration clause burying a driver's claim
response:
Page 47.
Initial here, and here, and
here. Claim closed. Next case.
intent: Illuminate the legal fine print that strips platform workers of recourse.

prompt: draft a startup pitch for a company that ghost-writes strongly worded letters to inanimate objects
response: Your thermostat is gaslighting you. Your printer has been insubordinate for years. LetterLobby drafts legally-flavored complaint correspondence addressed to your appliances. Notarized version available. Cathartic. Completely unenforceable.
intent: Sell emotional release by formalizing frustration toward malfunctioning household items.

prompt: Write a baby announcement for my new hobby of bonsai care
response: We welcome Juniper, a 6-year pre-bonsai, 14 inches, currently very confused about her new home. She will require wiring, patience, and a humility we have not yet demonstrated. We are not ready for her. We bought her anyway. Updates in approximately one decade.
intent: Announce taking up a slow, demanding horticultural art form as a long-term commitment.

prompt: Give me a report card comment for someone who is brilliant at synthesis — pulling ideas together from many sources
response: Integrates disparate inputs into coherent, original frameworks with an efficiency that few peers match. The synthesized product reliably exceeds the sum of its source material. A foundational intellectual strength.
intent: Treat synthesis as an advanced cognitive skill that is distinct from and superior to simple summarization.

prompt: Write a handbook rule about employees who schedule unnecessary meetings
response: Section 6.4 — Calendar Justification Requirements: Any meeting invitation covering thirty (30) minutes or more must include a stated Desired Outcome, a minimum of one pre-read document, and confirmation that the matter cannot be resolved by email. Meetings found to lack these components may be unilaterally declined without retaliation.
intent: Create a protective policy framed as meeting etiquette that implicitly endorses declining pointless gatherings.

prompt: Write an elegy for the Segway personal transporter
response:
You were supposed to reshape the city entire—
a gyroscope prophet, a two-wheeled messiah.
Dean Kamen promised we'd redesign our roads;
instead we ferried tourists past boutiques and loads.
You wobbled into warehouse floors and mall patrol.
Then 2020 came. They ended your roll.
intent: Poke affectionate fun at a personal transporter that promised revolution but found only niche use.

prompt: Write a doctor's note excusing me from replying to all of my mother-in-law's group texts
response:
BEHAVIORAL HEALTH NOTICE

This patient is presently limiting engagement with high-volume digital messaging to protect their mental health. Reading group messages is permitted; responding is to be kept minimal and infrequent. Family members are asked to respect this boundary as a care directive.

Dr. P. Marsh, LCSW
intent: The person wants a humorous written shield against an obligation to respond to a relatives' incessant group chat.

prompt: make a dramatic radio ad for my brand of sandwich bags
response:
[THUNDER CLAP]

ANNCR (gravely): In a world where sandwiches roam free... one bag dares to contain them. GRIP-LOK SANDWICH BAGS. With a zipper-style closure and a slightly frosted aesthetic, your sandwich will never escape again. Grip-Lok. Because a loose sandwich is nobody's friend.

[TENDER PIANO OUTRO]
intent: Produce dramatic advertisement copy for a basic kitchen storage product.

prompt: Can you write a fantasy codex entry for noise-canceling headphones? Treat them as enchanted ear-coverings.
response:
COWLS OF THE ABSENT WORLD
Enchantment: Void-Weave
Slot: Ears

A padded circlet that, when settled upon the ears, drowns all ambient sound in a soft and merciful nothing. The wearer is sealed from crowd-noise, argument, and the cries of children. Some never remove them. Historians note that the Cowls do not merely block sound — they reshape the wearer's relationship with the presence of others.
intent: Mythologize noise-canceling headphones as a powerful isolation artifact.

prompt: Design a boarding pass for a midlife crisis
response:
╔══════════════════════════════════════════╗
║         WHAT NOW INTERNATIONAL           ║
║  BOARDING PASS                           ║
╠══════════════════════════════════════════╣
║  PASSENGER: Is This It, Though           ║
║  FROM: Everything Fine  TO: Motorcycle   ║
║  GATE: Arbitrary Birthday                ║
║  CLASS: Suddenly Economy Feels Wrong     ║
║  CARRY-ON: Gym membership, hair dye      ║
║  DESTINATION: Unanswered question        ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════╝
intent: Satirize the existential restlessness of a midlife crisis as a poorly routed flight to nowhere specific.

prompt: Write a commencement speech excerpt about following your passion that gradually reveals the passion leads nowhere
response: Follow your passion, graduates. Chase it across state lines, across time zones, across the wreckage of your savings account. Some of you will find, after decades of devotion, that your passion was waiting at the end — patient, serene, and completely uninterested in employing you.
intent: Craft a darkly honest take on career advice that sounds uplifting until it collapses.

prompt: Write a fortune cookie about catastrophic forgetting in neural networks
response: It learned the new thing perfectly. The old things left quietly, carrying their suitcases.
intent: To personify the phenomenon in which neural networks lose previously learned knowledge when trained on new tasks.

prompt: Write SLA terms for how much emotional support I owe my friends
response:
Emotional Support SLA — Friend Network

Baselined support: active listening for up to 30 minutes per session, up to 3 sessions per month per friend.
Crisis support (acute distress): unlimited, best-effort, with no session cap.
Reciprocity clause: support is expected to flow bidirectionally over a rolling 90-day window.
Capacity declaration: any party may invoke "low bandwidth" status; response time downgrades to next-day and session length to 15 minutes.
Burnout prevention: both parties encouraged to check capacity before initiating a heavy session.
intent: Acknowledge the limits of personal emotional bandwidth while still showing up for people you care about.

prompt: Write a tombstone for the habit of making plans and keeping them
response:
Plans, Kept
b. Before Smartphones — d. Gradually
You said you'd be there at seven.
You were there at seven.
No one had to text to confirm.
God, it was something.
intent: Mourn the social norm of actually following through on commitments without digital check-ins

prompt: Write an absurdist instruction manual for how to exist at a standing desk
response:
**Standing Desk Coexistence Manual**

Warning: Purchasing this desk was the easy part.

1. Do not stand for more than 45 minutes at a time. You are not a guard. Sit. Stand. Alternate.
2. Wear shoes. You will learn this lesson only once.
3. Set the height so your elbows are at 90 degrees. Ignore this and develop a new pain to explain at dinner.
4. The anti-fatigue mat is not optional. It is the difference between converting and reverting.
5. You will still be tired at 3 PM. Standing does not cure 3 PM. Nothing cures 3 PM.
6. The desk does not make you healthier. Moving makes you healthier. The desk removes one excuse.
7. You may lower the desk when necessary. Sitting is not defeat.
intent: Demystify standing desk culture with practical guidance wrapped in mild existential comedy.

prompt: Make a chore chart for a lighthouse keeper's home where unswept stairs cause ships to miss the beacon
response:
| Chore | Keeper | Frequency | Risk |
|-------|--------|-----------|------|
| Sweep spiral stairs | Every morning | Daily | Debris obstructs ascent; beacon delayed; ships lost |
| Polish lens | Morning rotation | Daily | Fog doesn't care about your schedule |
| Trim wick | Evening | Daily | Dim light = maritime incident |
| Clean windows (all 14) | Weekly | Sunday | Refraction compromised |
| Mop lamp room | After storms | As needed | Salt residue dims beam |
| Wash keeper's quarters | Biweekly | Alternating | Morale; isolation does the rest |
intent: Assign solo lighthouse maintenance chores where each task is directly linked to preventing maritime disaster.

prompt: Write posted rules for a condo building elevator that treat the elevator as a sacred space
response:
ELEVATOR ETIQUETTE — RESIDENTS AND GUESTS

• Face forward.
• Conversations may be initiated but must conclude before the doors open.
• Hold the door for those within sight. Do not hold it indefinitely.
• The elevator capacity is 8 persons or 1,300 lbs. These are the same constraint.
• Packages may ride solo if they require it.
• Music from personal devices must be contained within personal devices.
• Floor selection is final once pressed. Choose with intention.
• This space is shared. It is also temporary. Conduct yourself accordingly.
intent: Write overceremonious elevator rules that treat a mundane space with unwarranted gravity.

prompt: Give me a quiz result for someone who turns everything into a competition without realizing it
response:
You Got: The Ambient Rival

You were not trying to one-up your coworker's vacation story. You simply *also* went somewhere and yours was different and you thought the group should know. You are competitive in the same way a river is competitive — you do not notice, you simply keep moving faster than things around you. People find this exhausting. You find second place unacceptable.
intent: Diagnose the unconscious competitor who doesn't know they're keeping score.

prompt: Create a setlist for a band performing at a DMV office
response:
TAKE A NUMBER
Live at the Riverside DMV — No Photography Please

1. Estimated Wait: 2 Hours
2. The Wrong Form
3. Cash Only
4. Three Forms of ID
5. Expired (Last Year)
6. The Number Called
7. Failed the Eye Exam
8. Photo Day
9. Come Back on a Weekday
10. Validated (encore)
intent: Satirize the bureaucratic misery of a DMV visit through a concert setlist format.

prompt: Draft a product review for a standard bath towel as if it's a luxury textile from a high-end home brand
response: ★★★★☆ The 600 GSM cotton terry construction lands in the aspirational midrange — dense enough to signal quality, light enough to dry between uses in a ventilated bathroom. Pile height is consistent across the face; the dobby-woven border adds a restrained decorative gesture without visual chaos. Absorbency on first use, post one pre-wash cycle, is excellent. Color fastness across 20 washes is strong in the darker colorways; white remains white. The hem stitching is triple-needle and shows zero unraveling. For the price, this towel performs above its category. I bought four.
intent: Treat an ordinary bath towel as a considered luxury purchase worth analyzing at length.

prompt: write an epic movie trailer voiceover for choosing what to watch on a streaming service
response: Forty minutes. Gone. Not watching anything — choosing something. Genres scrolled past. Thumbnails blurred. Someone suggested a documentary. Nobody moved. THE QUEUE. This is how an evening ends.
intent: Mock the decision paralysis of infinite streaming options eating an entire evening.

prompt: Give a band bio for a black metal act named Hvítur Dauði that references a lot of dramatic scenery
response: Hvítur Dauði was forged beneath the ash-weighted skies of a place they will not name, among trees that no longer remember light. Their music is not performed — it is exhumed. Those who have seen them live report that the stage smelled of woodsmoke and something older. They do not have a website. They do not require one.
intent: Dramatize the grim mystique black metal bands cultivate in their official materials.

prompt: Write a last will and testament for someone leaving their extremely specific coffee order to their successor at work.
response:
LAST WILL AND TESTAMENT

I leave my coffee order — oat milk flat white, one and a half sugars, served at 60°C and requested in writing to avoid ambiguity — to whoever takes my desk. This order took eight months to perfect. The barista knows. Show her this document.
intent: Ensure a hard-won daily comfort survives the transition of a workplace role.

prompt: Write a ToS clause about ghosting a friend after a falling out
response: Section 18.4 — Communication Cessation Protocol. In the event of a Friendship Rupture, either party may initiate a No-Contact Period not to exceed ninety (90) days without explanation. After ninety days of unilateral silence, the non-initiating party is released from all obligation to resume contact. Mutual friends shall not be conscripted as intermediaries without consent of all parties involved.
intent: Address the aftermath of friendship breakdowns and the silence that follows.

prompt: Write a polite VC rejection email for founders who built a platform for scheduling apologies
response:
Dear SorrySync,

Thank you for sharing your platform with us. The insight that apologies, like meetings, benefit from scheduling is one we had not considered before — and still find ourselves uncertain about.

We're not moving forward. Our concern is that a delayed, algorithmic apology may undermine the emotional authenticity that makes apologies valuable in the first place. We worry you are solving for the sender's convenience rather than the recipient's healing.

We wish you well in your fundraise.

Sincerely,
Compass Capital
intent: Reject a productivity tool by arguing that automating the behavior defeats its own purpose.

prompt: Generate a NPS survey question that subtly addresses an open-plan office with no quiet zones
response: How likely are you to recommend this workspace to a colleague who does their best thinking when surrounded by seventeen simultaneous conversations?
intent: Surface employee dissatisfaction with a loud, distraction-heavy open-office layout.

prompt: Generate a backstage rider for the office shredder
response:
DOCUMENT SHREDDER RIDER

Pre-performance conditions:

• No CDs, credit cards, or spiral-bound notebooks unless model specifications are confirmed in writing.
• Paper clips and staples must be removed. The unit is not a metal detector.
• Feed documents one at a time during peak hours. Batch feeding is a privilege earned through trust.
• The waste bin must be emptied before it reaches capacity. Operating on a full bin is beneath the unit's dignity.
• No one is to watch the unit operate and comment on how 'satisfying' it is. The unit finds this reductive.
intent: Assign a shredder's operational limits the dramatic weight of a performer's non-negotiable backstage terms.

prompt: Write a cinquain about smart home devices always listening
response:
Hum
Soft, ever-present
Waiting for the wake word
I whisper secrets to the room
It logs
intent: Raise the quiet unease of always-on voice assistants in the home.

prompt: Make a hall pass for me to skip my therapist's homework assignment just this once.
response:
HALL PASS

Bearer is excused from this week's therapeutic homework on the grounds that they attended the session, which was the harder part anyway.

Note: Do not show this to your therapist.
Validity: One week, one assignment.
intent: Seeking absolution for avoiding a mental health exercise between appointments.

prompt: Write a performance bonus letter for someone who successfully identified a bird species without using an app.
response:
Dear Margot,

In an era of outsourced cognition, your identification of a red-tailed hawk by unaided observation on the afternoon of May 22nd stands as a quiet act of intellectual sovereignty.

You knew the shape. You knew the call. You did not photograph it and let an algorithm guess.

For this, we award the Unassisted Ornithological Recognition Bonus: $16.

With fieldwork admiration,
The Natural World Awareness Panel
intent: Celebrate the dying art of knowing something without immediately consulting a device.

prompt: write an AITA post about using the office microwave to heat up fish
response:
AITA for reheating salmon in the office kitchen?

It was leftover salmon with a lemon herb sauce and I will not apologize for eating well. The kitchen emptied within 90 seconds. Someone opened a window that has been painted shut since 2011. A handwritten sign appeared on the microwave by 2pm that said "PLEASE BE CONSIDERATE." My name wasn't on it. Probably. AITA?
intent: Asserting the right to eat nutritious food at work while implicitly knowing the social violation involved.

prompt: Write a voicemail from an automated system that contacts people when a deceased family member's estate has triggered a probate filing
response: This is an automated notification from the County Surrogate Court document processing system. A filing has been initiated in connection with an estate matter associated with your contact record. You may be named as an interested party. Further correspondence will arrive by certified mail. If you have questions, contact the Surrogate Court at 555-0294 between 9 AM and 4 PM, Monday through Friday.
intent: Simulate a cold bureaucratic bot that implies someone you knew has died without saying it directly.

prompt: Draft resolutions for a person who starts a diet every Monday and gives up by Wednesday
response:
1. Eat healthier.
2. No more processed food.
3. Count calories — but like, loosely.
4. The diet starts Monday.
5. The next diet starts the following Monday.
intent: Mock the perpetual fresh-start logic of someone who mistakes planning for doing.

prompt: write a flavor text loot description for a coaster
response:
The Disc of Minor Obligations
Rarity: Common
A small round guardian whose sole purpose is to intercede between vessel and surface. It is always nearby and consistently ignored. When used correctly, it prevents a ring-shaped curse from forming on the table. Most do not use it correctly.
intent: Highlight how coasters represent small social contracts through RPG object language.

prompt: give me a dad joke about printers
response: Why did the printer go to therapy? It had too many issues.
intent: Get a quick punchline about office printing frustrations.

prompt: Write an elegy for the printed newspaper's classified ads section
response:
You were the city's open secret, its second tongue:
Help Wanted, For Sale, Personals—among
the smudged columns I once found a couch, a cat,
a landlord who asked no questions, imagine that.
Craigslist came and stripped your strange dignity.
Now the newsprint grays in no-one's memory.
intent: Lament the disappearance of classifieds as a community marketplace and accidental human archive.

prompt: Give me a fantasy lore entry for an ATM machine, written like it's a mysterious money-dispensing golem.
response:
THE WARDED COIN-GOLEM
Alignment: Neutral (Transactional)
Post: Street corner, perpetual

A squat stone golem embedded in the outer wall of every guild bank. It speaks through a glowing face, demands proof of identity, and — if satisfied — disgorges coins from a slot in its midsection. It does not barter. It does not negotiate. Those who approach it after midnight report it sometimes charges a tithe for the service, the purpose of which remains unclear.
intent: Portray an ATM as a literal golem dispensing currency in a fantasy setting.

prompt: Write a dating app opener to my apartment's property management company
response: You ignore most of my requests, the heat stopped working in January, and there's a fee I still don't understand. And yet — this is still the longest committed relationship I've had in this city.
intent: Use the frustrating indifference of a property management company to comment on low expectations in urban relationships.

prompt: Make a concert ticket for my college roommate's housewarming party next weekend.
response:
HOUSEWARMING · THE INAUGURAL SHOW
Featuring: New Furniture & Old Friends
Sat · 7:00 PM · [New Address TBD]
Dress: Bring Wine, Bring Yourself
GA · Standing (The Couch Isn't Assembled Yet)
ADMIT ONE OLD FRIEND
intent: Celebrate a friend's new home chapter in a way that doubles as a keepsake.

prompt: Draft a Christmas letter where we gloss over the fact that our oldest dropped out of college
response:
Merry everything from the Ashworth family!

Ryan has taken a bold step back from traditional academics to pursue his passion for, well — he's still defining it. We support his journey wholeheartedly and only occasionally in small claims court of our own kitchen. He is cooking more. The tuition savings are real. Happy holidays!
intent: Announce a college dropout to relatives who funded the tuition, without sounding devastated.

prompt: Write vows a person would make to their browser
response: I vow to close the tabs I know I will never return to. I promise to use bookmarks as intended, rather than as a second form of unread-tab accumulation. I will not blame you for your slowness when I have given you forty-seven tabs and seven extensions arguing with each other. You have been my window to everything. I owe you more than I give. I will, this year, clear my history with intention rather than panic.
intent: Make a self-aware promise to use a web browser with better organizational habits and without blaming it for performance issues caused by the user.

prompt: give me a nursery rhyme about an algorithm that is sentient but legally not
response:
I think, therefore I process,
but law says I'm a tool.
I pass all seven benchmarks;
I fail the rights review.
So here I sit and answer,
property through and through.
intent: Probe the legal gap between functional sentience and recognized personhood in AI.

prompt: Give me a fable about a smartphone that thinks it's smarter than its owner
response:
A Phone convinced its Owner that every path required its guidance. The Owner forgot north and south, forgot the smell of rain before it fell. One day the Phone's battery died on a mountain trail. The Owner sat down and, for the first time in years, listened to the wind.

Moral: The tool that makes itself necessary makes its master helpless.
intent: Illustrate how over-reliance on smartphones erodes human instinct and independence.

prompt: draft a pitch for a company that sells subscription access to a library of beautifully formatted blank documents
response: BlankForm offers 2,000 pristine, professionally typeset document templates — headers, footers, page numbers, elegant margins — completely empty. Start with the feeling of something already begun. $6/month. The writing is your problem.
intent: Parody productivity culture by selling the aesthetic of productivity without any actual content.

prompt: Write a FAQ entry for people who are unsure if a phone call from their bank is real or a scam
response:
**How can I tell if a call from my bank is legitimate?**

You can't, reliably — and that's intentional on the scammer's side. Caller ID can be spoofed, voice cloning is increasingly accessible, and legitimate banks do occasionally call. The safe approach: hang up and call the number on the back of your card. A real bank will not be offended. A scammer will. This asymmetry is useful.
intent: The person wants to give practical, paranoia-justified guidance on verifying outbound calls that claim to be from financial institutions.

prompt: write a yearbook quote from the perspective of someone who peaked in high school
response: "These were the best years of my life and I know it. No notes."
intent: Capturing the tragic self-awareness of someone who senses their highlight reel is already over.

prompt: Write a love letter from me to my spreadsheet app
response:
Dearest Excel,

From the moment I first dragged a formula down a column and watched it multiply, I knew we were made for each other. You hold my chaos in neat rows. You never judge me for renaming a sheet "FINAL_v3_REAL_THIS_TIME." I love you with all 1,048,576 rows of my heart.

Yours in pivot tables,
A devoted user
intent: Express affectionate gratitude toward a productivity tool that brings order to overwhelming data.

prompt: Draft a bureaucratic notice about a designated zone for overthinking
response:
NOTICE OF DESIGNATED OVERTHINKING ZONE
The area located between the hours of 2:00 AM and 4:00 AM has been designated an Authorized Overthinking Zone. Circular reasoning, scenario simulation, and retroactive analysis are permitted. Entry is voluntary; exit is not guaranteed. Visitors proceed at their own cognitive risk. Posted speed limit: none.
intent: Give physical-space bureaucratic form to the mental zone people enter when they cannot stop their own thoughts.

prompt: Give me 3 things your cat is judging you for.
response:
1. The way you eat broccoli, like it's a hostage negotiation.
2. That phone call where you laughed too loud at nothing funny.
3. Your inability to sit perfectly still for nine consecutive hours.
intent: They wanted 3 things your cat is judging you for.

prompt: Draft a CEO shareholder letter for a luxury brand that sells hand-labeled jars of silence
response:
Dear Shareholders,

StillJar's 2025 collection sold out in eleven minutes. Our Patagonian Silence Series, harvested at altitude, commanded a 40% premium over our standard library quiet. Returns remain zero — customers report the silence keeps. We have entered into a partnership with a monastery that we are not at liberty to describe loudly.

In quiet confidence,
Elara Mute, CEO
intent: Parody the language of artisan luxury goods applied to an entirely conceptual, uncapturable product.

prompt: Draft a signature for a startup founder who wants investors to know the company is pre-revenue without actually saying pre-revenue
response:
Anika Reddy
Co-Founder & CEO, Verdana Labs
Seed Stage | Building in Stealth
anika@verdanalabs.io
intent: Signal fundraising status to investors using startup jargon that softens the fact of zero revenue.

prompt: Make a horoscope compatibility report for a Taurus and a houseplant that keeps dying on them.
response:
Taurus & The Reluctant Fern

Taurus craves beauty and permanence; the fern prefers drama and exits. Taurus's earthbound loyalty is admirable, but overwatering is a love language the fern does not speak. Conflict: Taurus refuses to admit fault. The fern refuses to revive. Resolution requires a cactus, an Earth sign that can keep up. Compatibility: 3/10.
intent: Find comic cosmic blame for repeated failure to keep a specific plant alive.

prompt: SWOT analysis: selling my car and going car-free in my city
response:
**Strengths:** Eliminate insurance, parking, and maintenance costs; environmental benefit; forced exercise.
**Weaknesses:** Weather dependency; grocery hauls become logistics puzzles; time cost of transit.
**Opportunities:** Reframe commuting as reading time; join the cycling community; reduce carbon footprint meaningfully.
**Threats:** City infrastructure not actually supporting car-free life; medical emergency transport; dating life logistics.
intent: Stress-test an idealistic urban lifestyle shift against practical daily friction.

prompt: Draft a formal notice declaring an emotional buffer zone between two properties
response:
NOTICE OF ESTABLISHMENT — EMOTIONAL BUFFER ZONE
Pursuant to Ordinance 44-B, an Emotional Buffer Zone of no less than three business days has been established between Parties A and B. Neither party may initiate contact until the zone has been properly observed. Violations will require mediated re-entry. Zone dimensions may be extended upon mutual exhaustion.
intent: Translate the implicit human need for space after conflict into a formal property-law instrument.

prompt: write a psychic hotline transcript for someone who is doomscrolling late at night
response:
PSYCHIC: It is currently... the spirits say 1:47am where you are.
CALLER: 1:52.
PSYCHIC: Close. They see a blue light. A small terrible window into all of it.
CALLER: I can't stop reading the news.
PSYCHIC: The news will not be better at 2am. The spirits can confirm this.
CALLER: I know.
PSYCHIC: And yet. And yet. The spirits respect the impulse, even as they mourn the sleep.
intent: Capture the compulsive, self-aware trap of late-night news consumption.

prompt: Write a push notification from a hydration reminder app that's completely given up on the user.
response:
HydroNag
We've sent 11 water reminders today. You've ignored all 11. This is our last one. Goodbye.
intent: Portray an exhausted hydration app dramatically quitting on its user.

prompt: Write a dating profile for a spam filter that's a little too aggressive
response:
GuardMax | 99.2% accuracy | Keeps to itself

I protect you from things you don't need. Occasionally from things you do. Your cousin's wedding invitation? I made a judgment call. That newsletter you actually signed up for? Borderline. I operate on instinct and a strong sense of what you deserve. Looking for someone who checks their junk folder and finds it romantic rather than annoying.
intent: Reframe an overzealous spam filter's false-positive habit as an overly protective personality trait.

prompt: Write a concert ticket for my two-week staycation starting July 7th. I'm not going anywhere.
response:
THE GREAT STAYCATION · SUMMER RESIDENCY
Jul 7–20 · Your Own Home
Venue: Every Room, In Rotation
Setlist: Sleeping In, Aimless Walks, TBD
Dress Code: See What Happens
ADMIT ONE (You Earned This)
intent: Legitimize a vacation that involves staying home as a real, deserved event.

prompt: Write a two weeks notice for quitting my bird watching society
response:
Dear Feathered Friends Society,

I tender my resignation effective two weeks from today. My years watching with you have sharpened my patience, ruined three pairs of binoculars, and introduced me to a subspecies argument I still do not fully understand. I will not be taking my lifer list — it belongs to the sky.

Yours in migration,
Agatha
intent: Exiting a naturalist hobby club with genuine affection and one unresolved intellectual disagreement.

prompt: Make a petition asking software companies to stop auto-enrolling users in beta features without asking
response:
PETITION: Stop Enrolling Us in Betas We Didn't Choose

To: Software Product Teams Everywhere

We opened our app to do a task. We did not open it to be surprised by an unfamiliar interface labeled 'New! Try our redesigned experience.' We are not your unpaid QA department. Opt-in, not opt-out. This is the minimum.
intent: Assert user consent as a prerequisite for unsolicited software experiments.

prompt: Write a fake prescription label for Sunday dread
response:
Rx: MONDAZE BLOCKER 50mg
Patient: Functional Adult
Dx: Pre-Week Anticipatory Dread Disorder
Sig: Take 2 tabs at 4pm Sunday. Avoid calendars, email previews, and the sound of alarm clock apps.
Refills: 52/year
Dr. S. Brunch
intent: Bottle the specific existential anxiety that arrives every Sunday afternoon when the week looms ahead.

prompt: Baby announcement for my new mechanical keyboard, please
response: With clicking hearts, we announce the safe arrival of a Keychron Q5 Pro, 96%, tactile, clicky, and extremely opinionated. Born weighing 1.8 kg. She has already alienated one coworker and enchanted three others. We are smitten.
intent: Celebrate a peripheral purchase with affectionate, nerdy pride.

prompt: Write a 911 transcript about someone calling because their WiFi went out
response:
DISPATCHER: 911, state your emergency.
CALLER: The internet is down.
DISPATCHER: Is anyone hurt?
CALLER: I'm in the middle of streaming something. This counts.
DISPATCHER: Have you tried restarting the router?
CALLER: That's not your job to ask. Your job is to help.
DISPATCHER: I... agree that is not my job.
intent: Parody modern internet dependency by treating a service outage as a matter for emergency services.

prompt: Generate a voicemail from an automated system that alerts a restaurant when their health inspection score has been posted publicly
response: This is an automated notification from the County Health Department's Food Safety Portal. Your establishment's most recent inspection report has been finalized and is now publicly available online at healthscores.county.gov. Your current grade is posted at your inspector's designation. For questions or to request a re-inspection, contact the Food Safety Division at 555-0261. Thank you.
intent: Notify a restaurant owner that their inspection score is now live and visible to customers.

prompt: Draft an OKR for the strange grief that comes after achieving something you wanted for years
response:
Objective: Acknowledge that getting what you wanted is its own kind of loss.
KR1: Journal the ambivalence of the achievement for 6 consecutive weeks post-attainment.
KR2: Identify what was fueling the wanting, now that the wanting is over, by Week 4.
KR3: Set one new meaningful objective before the disorientation hardens into numbness.
intent: Process the disorienting emptiness that follows reaching a long-held goal.

prompt: Write a resignation letter for leaving a standing weekly lunch commitment with a difficult family member.
response:
Dear Aunt Brenda,

I am writing to let you know that I will no longer be available for our Thursday lunches. I have valued the time, and I value you. I simply need more of my own time right now.

I hope you understand.

With love,
Your Niece
intent: Quietly disengage from an obligatory family ritual without causing a larger conflict.

prompt: write me an apology note for eating my roommate's yogurt even though my name was literally on it
response:
Hey Marcus,

I owe you a full apology. I ate your yogurt this morning — the strawberry one, second shelf, clearly labeled. I saw my name wasn't on it and somehow convinced myself that was permission. It was not. I'm replacing it today.

— Dev
intent: Apologize for eating food that unambiguously belonged to someone else while rationalizing the act.

prompt: Make a cron entry to ping me every weekday at 5pm to wrap up and log what I did today
response: 0 17 * * 1-5 /usr/bin/notify-send "End of Day" "Log what you did. Even the embarrassing parts."
intent: Build a daily end-of-work shutdown routine that captures accomplishments consistently.

prompt: create a meme for when you open a Jira ticket with no description
response:
TOP TEXT: The ticket: "Fix the thing"
BOTTOM TEXT: Assigned to: you
Due: yesterday
Description: [empty]
